[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:imported Kofun]]Tsuruki Kofun Cluster[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]][[en:Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Kyoto, Japan}} {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}} is a [[Kofun]] cluster located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].
It is dominated by {{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1" />
The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-26_scale:10000|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}''|前方後方墳}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping [[paddy field|rice fields]] on the west bank of the [[Kizu River]]. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving [[grave goods]] are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary [[Emperor Keitai]], and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an [[Edo period]] fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ōsumi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Katamachi Line]] ([[Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Kyoto, Japan}} {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}} is a [[Kofun]] cluster located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].
It is dominated by {{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1" />
The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-26_scale:10000|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}''|前方後方墳}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping [[paddy field|rice fields]] on the west bank of the [[Kizu River]]. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving [[grave goods]] are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary [[Emperor Keitai]], and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an [[Edo period]] fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ōsumi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Katamachi Line]] ([[Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Kyoto, Japan}} {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}} is a [[Kofun]] cluster located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].
It is dominated by {{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1" />
The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-26_scale:10000|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}''|前方後方墳}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping [[paddy field|rice fields]] on the west bank of the [[Kizu River]]. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving [[grave goods]] are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary [[Emperor Keitai]], and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an [[Edo period]] fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ōsumi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Katamachi Line]] ([[Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Kyoto, Japan}} {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}} is a [[Kofun]] cluster located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].
It is dominated by {{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1" />
The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-26_scale:10000|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}''|前方後方墳}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping [[paddy field|rice fields]] on the west bank of the [[Kizu River]]. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving [[grave goods]] are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary [[Emperor Keitai]], and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an [[Edo period]] fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ōsumi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Katamachi Line]] ([[Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Kyoto, Japan}} {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}} is a [[Kofun]] cluster located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].
It is dominated by {{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1" />
The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-26_scale:10000|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}''|前方後方墳}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping [[paddy field|rice fields]] on the west bank of the [[Kizu River]]. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving [[grave goods]] are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary [[Emperor Keitai]], and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an [[Edo period]] fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ōsumi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Katamachi Line]] ([[Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It is dominated by {{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1" />
The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Kyoto, Japan}} {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}} is a [[Kofun]] cluster located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].
It is dominated by {{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1" />
The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-26_scale:10000|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}''|前方後方墳}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping [[paddy field|rice fields]] on the west bank of the [[Kizu River]]. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving [[grave goods]] are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary [[Emperor Keitai]], and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an [[Edo period]] fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ōsumi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Katamachi Line]] ([[Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-26_scale:10000|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}''|前方後方墳}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping [[paddy field|rice fields]] on the west bank of the [[Kizu River]]. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving [[grave goods]] are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary [[Emperor Keitai]], and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an [[Edo period]] fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ōsumi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Katamachi Line]] ([[Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Kyoto, Japan}} {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}} is a [[Kofun]] cluster located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].
It is dominated by {{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1" />
The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.

It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Kyoto, Japan}} {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}} is a [[Kofun]] cluster located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].
It is dominated by {{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1" />
The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-26_scale:10000|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}''|前方後方墳}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping [[paddy field|rice fields]] on the west bank of the [[Kizu River]]. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving [[grave goods]] are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary [[Emperor Keitai]], and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an [[Edo period]] fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ōsumi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Katamachi Line]] ([[Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kankou-kyotanabe.jp/tourism/ohsumi_kurumazuka_tumulus/ Kyotanabe City Tourism Association home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.kyotanabe.lg.jp/0000018553.html Kyotanabe home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-26_scale:10000|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping [[paddy field|rice fields]] on the west bank of the [[Kizu River]]. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving [[grave goods]] are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary [[Emperor Keitai]], and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an [[Edo period]] fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ōsumi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Katamachi Line]] ([[Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]][[Category:前方後方墳]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Kyoto, Japan}} {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}} is a [[Kofun]] cluster located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].
It is dominated by {{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1" />
The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-26_scale:10000|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping [[paddy field|rice fields]] on the west bank of the [[Kizu River]]. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving [[grave goods]] are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary [[Emperor Keitai]], and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an [[Edo period]] fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ōsumi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Katamachi Line]] ([[Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]][[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:New]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Kyoto, Japan}} {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}} is a [[Kofun]] cluster located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].
It is dominated by {{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1" />
The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-26_scale:10000|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping [[paddy field|rice fields]] on the west bank of the [[Kizu River]]. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving [[grave goods]] are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary [[Emperor Keitai]], and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an [[Edo period]] fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ōsumi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Katamachi Line]] ([[Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-26_scale:10000|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping [[paddy field|rice fields]] on the west bank of the [[Kizu River]]. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving [[grave goods]] are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary [[Emperor Keitai]], and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an [[Edo period]] fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ōsumi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Katamachi Line]] ([[Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]][[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Kyoto, Japan}} {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}} is a [[Kofun]] cluster located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].
It is dominated by {{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1" />
The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-26_scale:10000|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping [[paddy field|rice fields]] on the west bank of the [[Kizu River]]. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving [[grave goods]] are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary [[Emperor Keitai]], and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an [[Edo period]] fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ōsumi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Katamachi Line]] ([[Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]][[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Kyoto, Japan}} {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}} is a [[Kofun]] cluster located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].
It is dominated by {{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1" />
The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-26_scale:10000|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping [[paddy field|rice fields]] on the west bank of the [[Kizu River]]. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving [[grave goods]] are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary [[Emperor Keitai]], and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an [[Edo period]] fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ōsumi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Katamachi Line]] ([[Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-26_scale:10000|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping [[paddy field|rice fields]] on the west bank of the [[Kizu River]]. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving [[grave goods]] are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary [[Emperor Keitai]], and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an [[Edo period]] fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ōsumi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Katamachi Line]] ([[Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Kyoto, Japan}} {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}} is a [[Kofun]] cluster located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].
It is dominated by {{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1" />
The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-26_scale:10000|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping [[paddy field|rice fields]] on the west bank of the [[Kizu River]]. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving [[grave goods]] are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary [[Emperor Keitai]], and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an [[Edo period]] fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ōsumi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Katamachi Line]] ([[Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:前方後方墳]][[sn:Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]][[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kyoto, Japan}} {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}} is a [[Kofun]] cluster located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]].
It is dominated by {{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1" />
The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-26_scale:10000|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping [[paddy field|rice fields]] on the west bank of the [[Kizu River]]. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving [[grave goods]] are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary [[Emperor Keitai]], and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an [[Edo period]] fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ōsumi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Katamachi Line]] ([[Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E|region:JP-26_scale:10000|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping [[paddy field|rice fields]] on the west bank of the [[Kizu River]]. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving [[grave goods]] are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary [[Emperor Keitai]], and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an [[Edo period]] fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from [[Ōsumi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Katamachi Line]] ([[Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>
