== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.83825|N|135.7441388888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kyotanabe|WD=Q816728}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtanabe, Kyoto|Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice fields}} on the west bank of the {{ill|Kizu River|en|Kizu River|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River}}. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}}, and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Ōsumi Station|en|Ōsumi Station|WD=Q589844|lt=Ōsumi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Katamachi Line|en|Katamachi Line|WD=Q1073379|lt=Katamachi Line}} ([[:en:Gakkentoshi Line|Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|WD=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kankou-kyotanabe.jp/tourism/ohsumi_kurumazuka_tumulus/ Kyotanabe City Tourism Association home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.kyotanabe.lg.jp/0000018553.html Kyotanabe home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]][[ja:大住車塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]][[ja:大住車塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]][[ja:大住車塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q104093003]] Wikidata name: Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Kyoto, Japan}} {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}} is a [[Kofun]] cluster located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtanabe, Kyoto|Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.
It is dominated by {{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1" />
The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.

It is located in the gently sloping {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice fields}} on the west bank of the {{ill|Kizu River|en|Kizu River|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River}}. The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.83825|N|135.7441388888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kyotanabe|WD=Q816728}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtanabe, Kyoto|Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice fields}} on the west bank of the {{ill|Kizu River|en|Kizu River|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River}}. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}}, and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Ōsumi Station|en|Ōsumi Station|WD=Q589844|lt=Ōsumi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Katamachi Line|en|Katamachi Line|WD=Q1073379|lt=Katamachi Line}} ([[:en:Gakkentoshi Line|Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|WD=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}}
==References== {{reflist}}

The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.83825|N|135.7441388888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kyotanabe|WD=Q816728}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtanabe, Kyoto|Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice fields}} on the west bank of the {{ill|Kizu River|en|Kizu River|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River}}. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}}, and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Ōsumi Station|en|Ōsumi Station|WD=Q589844|lt=Ōsumi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Katamachi Line|en|Katamachi Line|WD=Q1073379|lt=Katamachi Line}} ([[:en:Gakkentoshi Line|Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)|WD=Q20983065|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyoto)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://kankou-kyotanabe.jp/tourism/ohsumi_kurumazuka_tumulus/ Kyotanabe City Tourism Association home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.kyotanabe.lg.jp/0000018553.html Kyotanabe home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The Ōsumi Minamizuka Kofun to the west has the same shape and size, but is not part of the National Historic Site designation. On November 10, 2022, the {{nihongo|[[Yahatanishi Kurumazuka Kofun]]|八幡西車塚古墳||}}, {{nihongo|[[Tenriyama Kofun Cluster]]|天理山古墳群||}}, and the {{nihongo|Iioka Kurumazuka Kofun|飯岡車塚古墳||}} were additionally designated to the National Historic Site, and the name of the site was officially changed to the {{nihongo|[[Tsuruki Kofun Cluster]]|綴喜古墳群||}}.
== Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun == {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | native_name =大住車塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Osumi Kurumazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Kyoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.83825|N|135.7441388888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kyotanabe|WD=Q816728|1=Kyōtanabe}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.3rd century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|50|18|N|135|44|53|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun'''|大住車塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Ōsumi-Hachiōji neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kyōtanabe, Kyoto|Kyōtanabe, Kyoto]] in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1974.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1731|title= 大住車塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |access-date=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
The Ōsumi Kurumazuka Kofun is a {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like two co-joined rectangles when viewed from above. It is located in the gently sloping {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=rice fields|1=Paddy field}} on the west bank of the {{ill|Kizu River|en|Kizu River|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizu River|1=Kizu River}}. It was built during the early to middle Kofun period, from the end of the 4th century to the beginning of the 5th century. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 65 meters, the width at the rear is approximately 35 meters, and the width at the front facing northwest is approximately 18 meters. There are traces of a moat around the periphery, and the outer circumference is a rectangle approximately 60 meters wide and approximately 100 meters long. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' has been confirmed on the mound. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is thought to be a pit-style stone chamber containing a clay coffin, but as its has not been excavated, surviving {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} are unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4-311-75040-4}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> According to legend, this is the tomb of the eighth son of the legendary {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}}, and it has been subsequently suspected that this legend was an {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} fabrication.
The tumulus is about a ten-minute walk from {{ill|Ōsumi Station|en|Ōsumi Station|WD=Q589844|lt=Ōsumi Station|1=Ōsumi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Katamachi Line|en|Katamachi Line|WD=Q1073379|lt=Katamachi Line|1=Katamachi Line}} ([[:en:Gakkentoshi Line|Gakkentoshi Line]]).<ref name="Shiseki"/>
